# graft — full LLM summary

graft is a Go CLI that keeps one canonical, provider-neutral agent definition in sync across ten AI-coding providers. The canonical store is the source of truth; providers hold projections of it. Sync is bidirectional: a change in any provider is pulled back to canonical and pushed out to the rest. Status: in development. Anything not implemented is marked "planned" in the docs.

## Core model

- Canonical: provider-neutral agent under .graft/agents/<name>/.
- Provider: a target tool with its own layout/schema.
- EntryGate: the single object the CLI talks to (holds store + engine + locks). CLI never calls store/engine/providers directly.
- Workspace identity: tuple (root, remote, branch). One .graft/ tree per workspace row. Same branch in another worktree = same workspace.
- Sync run: one tracked execution with a run_id, recorded in sqlite.
- git mode: tracked (uses your real git repo + current branch as base) or internal (graft keeps its own repo when no git exists; migrates to tracked when real git appears, only at sync time — no git hooks).

## Canonical store format

.graft/agents/<name>/
  agent.yaml        canonical fields (provider-neutral)
  instructions.md   body / system prompt
  .meta.json        per-provider source hash + last commit hash

Frozen contract fields (CanonicalAgent in internal/contract):
name (string), description (string), model (string), tools ([]string), mcp ([]string), permissions (map[string]string), providerOverrides (map[provider]map), body (string, = instructions.md content).
providerOverrides preserves provider-specific values with no canonical home so sync stays lossless across round-trips. Exact agent.yaml schema owned by internal/canonical, derived from common-agent-definition.schema.json.

## Providers (ten, frozen ids)

claude-code, codex, gemini-cli, cursor, github-copilot, opencode, roo-code, goose, grok-cli, antigravity.
Each is its own package implementing the Provider interface: Name, Detect(root), Parse(path), ToCanonical(p), Serialize(a), Schema(). Implemented one at a time; per-provider docs land as each ships.

## CLI surface (plan 03; CLI reference is a stub auto-generated from cobra after phase h)

- graft init — create .graft/, register workspace row, detect git mode. Output: created path.
- graft agent list — list canonical agents + provider coverage. Output: table.
- graft agent <name> status — drift of one agent across providers.
- graft agents status — aggregated drift: out-of-sync providers + agent counts.
- graft sync agent <name> — sync one agent. Output: run_id + result.
- graft sync agents — sync all changed. Flags (planned): --dry-run, --continue.
- graft validate [--all | --provider <id>] — schema + semantic validation; non-zero exit on error findings. Runs implicitly before every sync.
- graft config get/set — read/write config.

## Config (global; koanf, internal/config)

- sync.gitAuto (bool): auto-commit tracking branches vs builtin-git only.
- scope (string, default "agents"; "skills"/"slash" planned): which capability is synced.
- providers.enabled[] ([]string): subset of the ten providers participating.

## How sync works (engine)

1. Detect & diff working tree vs base (current branch).
2. Branch per changed file: graft/<run_id>/agent/<name>.
3. Canonicalize each changed provider file -> .graft/agents/<name>.
4. Merge loop: merge per-file branches sequentially into a moving beta branch graft/<run_id>/beta/<n>.
5. Conflict -> record + surface path + stop (status=conflict, resumable). Resolve, then graft sync agents --continue resumes from recorded phase.
6. Reapply onto moving base: if base moved during run, redo onto beta_y; else proceed.
7. Copy stabilized beta tree into working dir. NO commit to base branch; beta is only a tracked reference.
8. Serialize canonical out to every enabled provider; prune temp branches.

Resumability: run row stores phase + beta_branch in sqlite. Concurrency: exclusive lock per workspace (root, remote, branch); second sync waits or resumes. One global sqlite db, WAL + locks.

## Drift / status

Per provider, graft stores a content hash (PROVIDER_LINK). Status recomputes the provider file hash and marks in_sync only when it matches both the stored hash and the canonical; else drifted. StatusReport: agents[] (per-provider in/out-of-sync + overall in_sync) and out_of_sync_providers (provider -> #agents drifted). Status is read-only.

## Validation

Validator.Validate(scope) -> []Finding{agent, provider?, path?, message, severity(error|warning)}. Errors block sync. EntryGate validates the changed scope before handing to the sync engine.

## Scope

In: ten providers, agents-first, bidirectional canonical<->provider, branch-per-file -> canonical merge -> conflict->manual -> reapply onto base with no commit, no-git internal repo with sync-time migration, concurrency-safe.
Out / planned: skills + slash-command sync, git hooks (excluded), TUI/web UI.

## Hosting

Docs are host-agnostic: docusaurus.config.ts url/baseUrl are placeholders, set at deploy time. No deploy workflow committed. Local offline search via @easyops-cn/docusaurus-search-local. Docs-only (blog disabled), TypeScript config, served at root.
